meaning: adj.无学问的,无教育的,不熟练的,天然的,没学好的n.没有文化的人们
examples: Thee enemy of art is unlearned.
But technology, once invented, cannot be unlearned.
Somewhere throughout the years we unlearned this important trait.
No scientist working on India's nuclear programme was under any illusion that bomb-making technology, once learned, could somehow be unlearned.
As usual, courage and cowardice is distributed among the rich and poor, the learned and unlearned, in a haphazard manner.
